author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XIII. 16–20.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have just heard in the holy Gospel the Lord speaking, and saying, “Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord, nor the apostle [he that is sent] greater than he that sent him: if ye know these things, blessed shall ye be if ye do them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said this, therefore, because He had washed the disciples’ feet, as the Master of humility both by word and example.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we shall be able, with His help, to handle what is in need of more elaborate handling, if we linger not at what is perfectly clear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, after uttering these words, the Lord added, “I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but, that the Scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me, shall lift up his heel upon me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is this, but that he shall trample upon me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know of whom He speaks: it is Judas, that betrayer of His, who is referred to.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had not therefore chosen the person whom, by these words, He setteth utterly apart from His chosen ones.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When I say then, He continues, “Blessed shall ye be if ye do them, I speak not of you all:” there is one among you who will not be blessed, and who will not do these things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I know whom I have chosen.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whom, but those who shall be blessed in the doing of what has been commanded and shown as needful to be done, by Him who alone can make them blessed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The traitor Judas, He says, is not one of those that have been chosen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is meant by what He says in another place, “Have I not chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it that he also was chosen for some purpose, for which he was really necessary; although not for the blessedness of which He has just been saying, “Blessed shall ye be if ye do these things”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He speaketh not so of them all; for He knows whom He has chosen to be associated with Himself in blessedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of such he is not one, who ate His bread in order that he might lift up his heel upon Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The bread they ate was the Lord Himself; he ate the Lord’s bread in enmity to the Lord: they ate life, and he punishment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For he that eateth unworthily,” says the apostle, “eateth judgment unto himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “From this time,” Christ adds, “I tell you before it come; that when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am He:” that is, I am He of whom the Scripture that preceded has just said, “He that eateth bread with me, shall lift up his heel upon me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He then proceeds to say: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send, receiveth me; and he that receiveth me, receiveth Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did He mean us to understand that there is as little distance between one sent by Him, and Himself, as there is between Himself and God the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we take it in this way, I know not what measurements of distance (which may God forbid!) we shall be adopting, in the Arian fashion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they, when they hear or read these words of the Gospel, have immediate recourse to their dogmatic measurements, whereby they ascend not to life, but fall headlong into death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they straightway say: The Son’s messenger stands at the same relative distance from the Son, as expressed in the words, “He that receiveth whomsoever I send, receiveth me,” as that in which the Son Himself stands from the Father, when He said, “He that receiveth me, receiveth Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou sayest so, thou forgettest, heretic, thy measurements.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if, because of these words of the Lord, thou puttest the Son at as great a distance from the Father as the messenger [apostle] from the Son, where dost thou purpose to place the Holy Spirit?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has it escaped thee, that ye are wont to place Him after the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will therefore come in between the messenger and the Son; and much greater, then, will be the distance between the Son and His messenger, than between the Father and His Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or perhaps, to preserve that distinction between the Son and His messenger, and between the Father and His Son, at their equality of distance, will the Holy Spirit be equal to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as little will ye allow this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where, then, do ye think of placing Him, if ye place the Son as far beneath the Father, as ye place the messenger beneath the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Restrain, therefore, your foolhardy presumption; and do not be seeking to find in these words the same distance between the Son and His messenger as between the Father and His Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But listen rather to the Son Himself, when He says, “I and my Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there the Truth hath left you no shadow of distance between the Begetter and the Only-begotten; there Christ Himself hath erased your measurements, and the rock hath broken your staircase to pieces.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now that the heretical slander has been disposed of, in what sense are we to understand these words of the Lord: “He that receiveth whomsoever I send, receiveth me; and he that receiveth me, receiveth Him that sent me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we were inclined to understand the words, “He that receiveth me, receiveth Him that sent me,” as expressing the oneness in nature of the Father and the Son; the sequence from the similar arrangement of words in the other clause, “He that receiveth whomsoever I send, receiveth me,” would be the unity in nature of the Son and His messenger.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there might, indeed, be no impropriety in so understanding it, seeing that a twofold substance belongeth to the strong man, who hath rejoiced to run the race; for the Word was made flesh, that is, God became man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And accordingly He might be supposed to have said, “He that receiveth whomsoever I send, receiveth me,” with reference to His human nature; “and he that receiveth me” as God, “receiveth Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in so speaking, He was not commending the unity of nature, but the authority of the Sender in Him who is sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let every one, therefore, so receive Him that is sent, that in His person he may give heed to Him who sent Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, thou lookest for Christ in Peter, thou wilt find the disciple’s instructor; and if thou lookest for the Father in the Son, thou wilt find the Begetter of the Only-begotten: and so in Him who is sent, thou art not mistaken in receiving the Sender.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What follows in the Gospel cannot be compressed within the shortness of the time remaining.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And therefore, dearly beloved, let what has been said, if thought sufficient, be received in a healthful way, as pasture for the holy sheep; and if it is somewhat scanty, let it be ruminated over with ardent desire for more.
